units.content: Code structure includes dark matter candidates, metric expansion, local void, evolving dark energy, Higgs, and Mach principle as optional theoretical components
Dark matter hypothesis evaluation compares halo mass to missing mass ratio
Dark matter particle detection status: null across multiple experiments (LZ, XENON, PandaX)
Metric expansion applies Hubble constant H0 to scale positions and momenta over time interval dt
Local void analysis computes predicted local H₀ from global H₀ and density contrast
Local underdensity (20-25%) observed by JWST COSMOS-Web could explain H₀ tension
Local void explanation is theoretical but if true reconciles conflicting measurements as both correct measurements of different volumes
Dark energy equation of state w(z) varies differently in DESI model versus ΛCDM constant
DESI model shows dark energy was stronger in past and weakening now, contrasting with ΛCDM constant
DESI dark energy hint is significant (2.8-4.2σ) but not yet settled, challenging ΛCDM
Higgs mass depends on Yukawa coupling constant scaled by vacuum expectation value (246.22 GeV)
Mach coupling function accumulates gravitational coupling strength from all other entities weighted by mass and distance
